channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Implementing Pollard's Kangaroo Algorithm on CUDA
item.description: Implementing Pollard's Kangaroo Algorithm on CUDA Pollard's Kangaroo is an algorithm for solving the elliptic curve discrete logarithm problem (ECDLP) when the private key is known to lie in a...

item.title: Get good at coding to stand out. No doubt! But your code won't speak for you. Here's what will:
item.description: Forget Syntax and Lines of Code. Do This to Stand Out Cesar Aguirre ・ Mar 9 #coding #beginners #career #careerdevelopment

item.description: A couple of weeks ago, Google and Samsung announced a big Gemini development coming to their newest devices: task automation. Starting with food delivery and rideshare apps, Gemini would be able...

item.title: 50% of Your Users Don't Have Eyes

item.title: Building a Crypto Payment Flow
item.description: If you’re building a digital product and want to accept cryptocurrency payments, the technical implementation is more straightforward than you might expect. Here’s a practical overview of how we...

item.title: I Built curl for Modbus
item.description: I spent three years managing 2,500 IoT fuel dispensing kiosks across Kenya and Rwanda. Every one of them had Modbus sensors: flow meters, level sensors, temperature probes, all talking RS485...

item.title: Rebuilding domenicotenace.dev: How Pure Astro and CSS Reminded Me That Simple Is Better 🌟
item.description: Overview Hey everyone 👋 I recently did something that felt almost rebellious in today's web development landscape: I completely refactored my personal website using only Astro and vanilla CSS. No...

item.title: Bumble to launch an AI dating assistant, ‘Bee’
item.description: Bumble's new AI assistant Bee will move the dating app beyond the swipe by matching people based on compatibility and goals.

item.title: From Curiosity to Contribution: My Journey with OWASP BLT and the Road to GSoC 2026
item.description: My journey into open source didn’t start with a perfectly planned goal or roadmap. Like many developers, I had been using open-source tools for years without really thinking about what it meant to...

item.title: Reproducibility of Analytical Decisions: Introducing a Deterministic Analytical Runtime
item.description: The problem of reconstructing analytical processes Modern analytical systems generate increasingly complex decisions through pipelines composed of data ingestion, transformations, models, and...

item.title: A writer is suing Grammarly for turning her and other authors into ‘AI editors’ without consent
item.description: Journalist Julia Angwin is leading a class action lawsuit against Grammarly for violating her privacy and publicity rights.

item.title: Google’s TV Streamer 4K doubles as a smart home hub and it’s on sale
item.description: Between March Madness and the Oscars, now might be a good time to improve your TV setup. Google’s TV Streamer 4K is currently on sale for $79.99 ($20 off) at Amazon, Target, and Walmart, which is...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude AI can respond with charts, diagrams, and other visuals now
item.description: Anthropic's latest update to Claude will allow the AI chatbot to generate custom charts, diagrams, and other visualizations during your conversation. If Claude determines a visual is useful based...

item.title: Gumloop lands $50M from Benchmark to turn every employee into an AI agent builder
item.description: As companies race to adopt AI, Benchmark general partner Everett Randle believes the key to success lies in empowering every worker with AI superpowers, and Gumloop’s intuitive agent builder is an...

item.title: Microsoft’s head of experiences and devices is retiring, triggering a shakeup
item.description: Rajesh Jha, executive vice president of Microsoft's experiences and devices group, is retiring after more than 35 years at the software giant. Jha was a big part of the team that helped move...

item.title: PEGI’s new age ratings will restrict FC 27 to kids over 16
item.description: The Pan-European Game Information (PEGI) body previously assigned its age ratings based on the presence of sex, drugs, violence, bad language, and fear. Now it's adding four new options that tie...

item.title: Alexa+ gets a new ‘adults only’ personality option that curses but won’t get into NSFW content
item.description: The new Sassy style can curse and roast you, but the fun ends there.

item.title: WordPress launches an in-browser website creator
item.description: WordPress.org is launching a new tool that lets you create a private website directly in your browser, according to an announcement on Wednesday. You can access the tool by heading to...

item.title: Wonderful raises $150M Series B at $2B valuation
item.description: The funding round, led by Insight Partners, comes just four months after Wonderful raised a $100 million Series A.

item.title: How AI is helping improve heart health in rural Australia
item.description: A doctor is sitting across a desk from a patient. The doctor is holding a tablet and a pen. Medical charts renderings are in the background.

item.title: Rivian R2 prices revealed: you’re going to have to wait longer for that $45,000 version
item.description: When Rivian first revealed the R2 in 2024, a lot of the excitement was centered on the starting price of $45,000 - placing it slightly below the current average selling price for a new internal...

item.title: Dyson’s new stain-spotting AI robovac is now available
item.description: Dyson's latest autonomous robot vacuum cleaner first debuted during IFA 2025 in Berlin last September. The Spot+Scrub Ai Robot is the company's follow-up to its 360 Vis Nav robovac launched in...

item.title: Honda cancels Zero Series EVs, citing ‘extremely challenging’ situation
item.description: Farewell, Honda Zero. We never knew thee. Honda is cancelling its Zero Series SUV and Saloon, as well as the Acura RSX EVs, citing an "extremely challenging earnings situation." The Japanese...

item.title: How to Scale Phishing Detection in Your SOC: 3 Steps for CISOs
item.description: Phishing has quietly turned into one of the hardest enterprise threats to expose early. Instead of crude lures and obvious payloads, modern campaigns rely on trusted infrastructure,...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: OAuth Trap, EDR Killer, Signal Phishing, Zombie ZIP, AI Platform Hack & More

item.title: Google is using old news reports and AI to predict flash floods
item.description: A new way to solve data scarcity: Turning qualitative reports into quantitative data with an LLM.
